title: alnus incana f. incisa
intro: Alnus incana f. incisa, also known as the narrow-leaved green alder, is a variety of the alder tree (Alnus incana) that is native to Europe, western Asia, and northern Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The narrow-leaved green alder has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing, due to its high content of salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Alnus incana f. incisa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and the plant is also used in traditional medicine to make salves and ointments for topical application.
